mission.title: helm: Adopt resources into release with correct instance and managed-by labels
mission.description:
Alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7627, https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625, and https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.

Help with validation would be appreciated, but in theory this closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6850, closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/4824, closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/2947, and closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/7418. This implementation would also help make https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/2730 v

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
KIND=deployment
NAME=my-app-staging
RELEASE=staging
NAMESPACE=default
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE
kubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
Related to https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6850, and an alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.

### Steps to Reproduce

Create a chart with

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1beta2
kind: Deployment
```

Deploy a release of that chart, then switch to apps/v1:

```diff
- apiVersion: apps/v1beta2
+ apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
```

Run `upgrade`, and you should get the following error before this patch:

```
rendered manifests contain a new resource that already exists. Unable to continue with update: existing resource conflict: namespace: default, name: version-migrate-test, existing_kind: apps/v1, Kind=Deployment, new_kind: apps/v1, Kind=Deployment
```
codeSnippets:
KIND=deployment
NAME=my-app-staging
RELEASE=staging
NAMESPACE=default
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE
kubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm

KIND=deployment
NAME=my-app-staging
RELEASE=staging
NAMESPACE=default
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE
kubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
Run `upgrade`, and you should get the following error before this patch:

See also discussion on labels in https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6365#issuecomment-547151315
thanks @jlegrone. I'm going to give this a spin today. Looks like there are some significant behavioural changes under the hood to `helm install` with the change to call `KubeClient.Update`, so I want to make sure we're not introducing any regressions here.
is this still a work-in-progress, or can we remove that label?
> is this still a work-in-progress, or can we remove that label?

@bacongobbler yes, see TODOs in the description. I think the main outstanding issue is to validate 3-way diff behavior, which is why I've kept the wip label for now.
A basic test looks promising!

I created two pods: `foo` and `bar`. One had the annotations, the other didn't.

Here's what I used for testing: [testchart.zip](https://github.com/helm/helm/files/4294581/testchart.zip)
